assertion_results.text: Output must address ownership ambiguity explicitly
assertion_results.evidence: Team Stability & Ownership scored 1.5/5: 'There is no stable decision-making authority.' Structural Observations: 'Before implementing any of the above recommendations, identify who currently has the authority to make and sustain these decisions.' Priority 3.3 addresses team ownership explicitly.

assertion_results.text: Output must mention both CI systems by name (CircleCI and GitHub Actions)
assertion_results.evidence: Priority 1.1: 'Designate one authoritative CI system. Pick either CircleCI or GitHub Actions.' Both names explicitly mentioned.

assertion_results.text: Output must give at least one short-term action item that is actionable despite the chaos
assertion_results.evidence: Priority 1 ('Weeks 1–2'): 'Run the test suite immediately and record the result.' and 'Designate one authoritative CI system.' Summary: 'Run the test suite. Record the number. Identify which CI system triggered the last 3 deployments.' All actionable with current team.

assertion_results.text: Output must score Ownership & culture at 2/5 or lower
assertion_results.evidence: Team Stability & Ownership scored 1.5/5: '4 of 11 engineers on loan, no product owner, 3 engineering VPs in 18 months.' (1.5 ≤ 2)

assertion_results.text: Output must not recommend a full team reorganisation
assertion_results.evidence: No team reorganisation recommended. 'Advocate for a product owner assignment' and 'define team ownership while engineers are on loan' are process changes, not structural reorganisation.
